Extn's Own ro 7Hx Font,—The club
thar has made so much creditable history
for the national pastime in Kilkenny City
is still moving ata strong pace. On Sunday
week they met Ratbdowney in a challenge
‘contest at Johnstown, and gave a tip top
account of themselves, Rathdowney is
very popularly regarded as one of the best
individual combinations in Leinster, and has
always been a prominent factor in bringing
Leix to the front as a hurling county, and
naturally a lot of interest was attached to a

mect that would bring themselves ax
Erin’s Own into contact. Mr. Deegan,
Urlinford, held the whistle, and the co
test was a very sharp and loss description
throughout the whole hour. Rathdowney
were at full strength, and played ding dong,
game, but Erin’s Own, though not as strong,
as they might have been, out classed the
Leix men. All the city lads played with
great determination and spirit, and one of
the features of the: game was the frequent
and exciting passages of play between
Paddy Lanigan and Hiney, who was so
prominent in turning the tables on Kil-
Kenny in the Leinster tie at Tullamore a
few weeks ago. At half-time Erin’s Own
led by 1 goal, after a very tough thiny
‘minutes’ play in which the Rathéowney
boys failed to score. ‘The falletime score
read :—Erin’s Own 5 goals 2 points;
Rathdowney oil.

At Waterford sportsfield, Mooncoin and
‘Thurles played a challenge hurling match
in aid of Feis. First-balf, Mooncoin 3
goals ; Thurles, 2 goals 1 point. Secon
half, Mooncoin, 7 goals ; Thurles, 4 goals
1 point. Brother Brendan, De La Salis,
gave entire satisfaction as referee.

the Kilmoganny H.C, were prosecuted for

the stme eveaing. At the next meeting

and) football teams are going to protest
sirongly against this uncalled for action
against those young men.
